title: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok down? Thousands report receiving ‘Your account is currently unavailable’ message
snippet: Facebook, TikTok and Instagram are down for users across the globe, with hundreds of people reporting outages. Users have reported receiving ‘Your account is currently unavailable due to a site issue’.

title: Apple hikes prices as iPhone maker unveils new MacBook Air and Pro versions — Check latest rates
snippet: Apple has updated its MacBook Air and MacBook Pro with faster processors, upgraded storage, and new external monitors. Prices have increased, with the Air starting at $1,099 and the Pro at $2,199. The new models support advanced features such as Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 6.

title: Oppo K14 5G India launch date announced: Check expected price, specs and more
snippet: The Oppo K14 5G is set to launch in India on March 9 at 12 noon. The phone will come with a 7,000 mAh battery ad support for 45W of fast charging. The device could come in the sub- ₹25,000 price bracket.&nbsp;

title: ‘Not worth it’: OpenAI researcher questions Pentagon amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend
snippet: An OpenAI researcher has expressed concerns about the company's recent deal to deploy AI tools for the US Department of Defense. The company has been facing backlash since it announced the partnership with Pentagon last week.

title: Nothing Phone 4a, 4a pro price and storage variants leaked ahead of launch: here's what to expect
snippet: A new leak has suggested that the Nothing Phone 4a could come with a 1.5K OLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, and a 50MP camera. The phone could begin at a price of  ₹31,999 while the &nbsp;4a Pro could start at  ₹40,000.

title: iPhone 17e vs Galaxy S25 FE vs Pixel 10a: Specs, features, India pricing and more compared
snippet: Apple launched the iPhone 17e with an A19 processor, priced under  ₹70,000. Competing with Pixel 10a and Galaxy S25 FE, it features a 6.1-inch display, IP68 rating, and a single 48MP camera. The Pixel 10a offers better battery and price, while the S25 FE has a more versatile camera setup.

title: Apple Launch event 2026: iPhone 17e price starts at  ₹64,990, upgrades base storage to 256 GB for budget buyers
snippet: &nbsp;The device features the A19 chip and MagSafe support. Pre-orders begin Wednesday, with sales starting March 11, as Apple aims to attract new users amid rising memory chip prices.

Sam Altman tells staff OpenAI has no say over Pentagon decisions
snippet: OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman told employees the company does not get to decide how the Department of Defense (DoD) uses its artificial intelligence software. He said the Pentagon will hear OpenAI’s expertise but will not allow it to make operational decisions, following tensions with Anthropic.

title: AI contract restrictions could threaten military missions: US official
snippet: A senior Pentagon official warned that broad operational limits in commercial AI contracts under the Biden administration could hinder real-time military planning and combat operations, with terms so restrictive that models might stop mid-mission. title: Anthropic nears $20 billion revenue run rate amid Pentagon feud
snippet: Anthropic is nearing $20 billion in annual run-rate revenue, up sharply from late 2025, driven by strong adoption of its AI models and Claude Code. Valued at $380 billion, the company shows rapid growth, though its clash with the Pentagon now casts doubt over business prospects.

title: Intel board chair Frank Yeary to depart after 17 years
snippet: Intel chair Frank Yeary will retire, with Craig Barratt set to succeed him. Yeary served on ⁠the board ‌since 2009 and was chair since 2023. He has presided over four CEO transitions and has dealt with ​the decline of Intel's ​manufacturing and the rise of TSMC.

title: State Department switches to OpenAI as US agencies start phasing out Anthropic
snippet: United States cabinet agencies and the military ordered staff to stop using Anthropic products, including Claude, after failed talks over military safeguards. Departments will switch to OpenAI and Google models. The move follows President Donald Trump’s directive and affects Pentagon contracts.

title: OpenAI is developing alternative to Microsoft's GitHub: The Information
snippet: OpenAI is developing a new code-hosting platform to rival GitHub after service disruptions. The early-stage project could be sold to customers, marking competition with Microsoft. A recent funding round valued OpenAI at $840 billion, showing strong AI investment interest.

title: Pentagon dispute bolsters Anthropic reputation but raises questions about AI readiness in military
snippet: Anthropic refused to let Claude be used for autonomous weapons, prompting the Trump administration to ban it from government use. Downloads rose as consumers backed its ethics. snippet: India’s beauty and personal care market is accelerating rapidly, thanks to rising personal care spending by young consumers. E-commerce and rapid delivery platforms are defining how beauty products are accessed.
